Name: Lusaka Industrial Manufacturing Bond 2025 – Series A
Description: 5-year corporate bond for Lusaka light manufacturing sector. Backed by equipment tokens and SMEAsset tokens from 18 registered manufacturers. Coupon: 11.75% p.a. PIA-approved. Listed on LuSE.

Name: Lusaka Industrial Manufacturing Bond 2025 – Series B
Description: 7-year manufacturing bond for Zambia’s food processing and packaging sector in Lusaka and Copperbelt. Backed by 22 tokenized production equipment assets. Coupon: 12.00% p.a. PIA-approved.

Name: Lusaka Industrial Manufacturing Bond 2025 – Series C
Description: 3-year short-duration bond for textile and garment manufacturers in Kabwe and Lusaka. Backed by inventory and equipment tokens. Coupon: 13.50% p.a. PIA-approved.

Name: Lusaka Industrial Manufacturing Bond 2025 – Series D
Description: 5-year bond for Zambia plastic and chemical product manufacturers. Backed by factory building land tokens and equipment. Coupon: 11.25% p.a. PIA-approved. CEEC-endorsed impact bond.

Name: Lusaka Industrial Manufacturing Bond 2025 – Series E (Women’s Impact)
Description: 5-year impact manufacturing bond exclusively for women-led manufacturing enterprises. Backed by 31 SMEAsset and Equipment tokens. Coupon: 10.50% p.a. PIA-approved. ZDA-certified impact bond.

Name: Zambia SME Invoice Finance ETF – Class A
Description: Exchange-traded fund backed by a pool of 350 verified trade invoice tokens from PACRA-registered SMEs. Weighted average yield: 13.5% p.a. Daily liquidity. Listed on LuSE. Management fee: 0.65% p.a.

Name: Zambia SME Invoice Finance ETF – Class B (Retail Sector)
Description: ETF tracking invoice tokens specifically from SMEs supplying major retail chains (Pick n Pay, Shoprite, Game). 210 invoice tokens in basket. Weighted yield: 12.8% p.a. Listed on LuSE.

Name: Zambia SME Invoice Finance ETF – Class C (Agriculture Supply Chain)
Description: ETF tracking invoice tokens from agri-SMEs supplying processors and exporters. 180 invoice tokens. Weighted yield: 14.2% p.a. Quarterly distribution. Listed on LuSE.

Name: Zambia SME Invoice Finance ETF – Class D (Construction Sector)
Description: ETF tracking payment claim and construction invoice tokens from NHA and ZNRA contractor SMEs. 92 invoice tokens. Weighted yield: 15.0% p.a. Listed on LuSE.

Name: Zambia SME Invoice Finance ETF – Class E (Diversified)
Description: Diversified ETF tracking invoices from SMEs in health, education, technology and logistics sectors. 145 invoice tokens. Weighted yield: 13.1% p.a. Listed on LuSE.

Name: Zambia Farmer Revenue Participation Note – Series 1 (Maize)
Description: Innovative revenue-linked note where investors participate in Eastern Province maize farmer revenue. Returns: 15–22% of crop revenue. 3-year term. Backed by HarvestForward tokens. SECZ-approved.

Name: Zambia Farmer Revenue Participation Note – Series 2 (Livestock)
Description: Revenue-linked note tied to beef and small-stock sales from Southern Province ranches. Revenue participation: 18–25%. 2-year term. Backed by Livestock tokens. SECZ-approved.

Name: Zambia Farmer Revenue Participation Note – Series 3 (Aquaculture)
Description: Revenue-linked note tied to tilapia and kapenta fish sales from Kafue and Bangweulu fisheries. Revenue participation: 20–28%. 2-year term. Backed by Aquaculture tokens. SECZ-approved.

Name: Zambia SME Revenue Participation Note – Series 4 (Bakery)
Description: Revenue-linked note tied to Lusaka Bakers Guild monthly sales. Investors receive 12% of monthly revenue up to target. 18-month term. Backed by Invoice tokens. SECZ-approved.

Name: Zambia Mining Revenue Participation Note – Series 5 (Royalty)
Description: Revenue-linked note tied to mineral royalty income from Copperbelt mines. Investors receive 10–15% of quarterly royalty token receipts. 5-year term. Backed by MineralRoyalty tokens. SECZ-approved.

Name: Zambia Agri-Food Supply Chain Finance Token – Maize Export SC
Description: Tokenized supply chain finance instrument for maize from farm to Zambian export processors. Covers 3,200 MT Eastern Province maize harvest. 90-day term. Zanaco collateral-backed. FIC-compliant.

Name: Zambia Agri-Food Supply Chain Finance Token – Soya Export SC
Description: Supply chain finance for soya from cooperative farms to Grain Traders Association processors. 1,800 MT of soya. 60-day revolving facility. FIC-compliant.

Name: Zambia Copper Value Chain Finance Token – Smelting SC
Description: Supply chain finance token covering ore transport, smelting and refining stage from mine to LME-registered copper warehouse. 120-day term. FIC-compliant.

Name: Zambia SME Retail Supply Chain Finance Token – Baking SC
Description: Supply chain finance instrument for Lusaka bakers from wheat/flour procurement to retail delivery. Covers 12 SMEs. 45-day revolving. Backed by supplier invoices. FIC-compliant.

Name: Zambia Fisheries Supply Chain Finance Token – Cold Chain SC
Description: Supply chain finance for tilapia and kapenta from Kafue fisheries cooperative to Lusaka cold storage and distribution. 30-day revolving facility. FIC-compliant.

Name: Zambia Crop Micro-Insurance Token – Maize Drought Cover (Eastern Province)
Description: Tokenized index-based crop micro-insurance policy covering 420 smallholder maize farmers in Eastern Province against drought. Payout triggered when rainfall index falls below threshold. Insured value: ZMW 1,200/farmer. FIC-compliant.

Name: Zambia Livestock Micro-Insurance Token – Cattle Death Cover (Southern Province)
Description: Tokenized livestock micro-insurance covering 280 cattle farmers against disease and drought-related herd death in Southern Province. Indemnity: ZMW 4,500/head. FIC-compliant.

Name: Zambia Fisheries Micro-Insurance Token – Aquaculture Flood Cover
Description: Tokenized micro-insurance covering 95 aquaculture farmers against flood destruction of fish ponds along Kafue River. Payout: ZMW 15,000/pond. FIC-compliant.

Name: Zambia SME Business Interruption Micro-Insurance Token
Description: Tokenized business interruption micro-insurance for 150 Lusaka SMEs covering fire, theft and equipment breakdown. Premium: ZMW 1,800/annum. Max payout: ZMW 50,000. FIC-compliant.

Name: Zambia Mining Community Micro-Insurance Token – Accident Cover
Description: Tokenized personal accident micro-insurance for 600 artisanal and small-scale miners in Copperbelt and North-Western Provinces. Benefit: ZMW 12,000 death + ZMW 8,000 disability. FIC-compliant.
